title: Noun
definition: A noun is a word that names a person, place, animal, thing, or idea.
formula: Noun = Name of a person / place / animal / thing / idea
rules: Common nouns name general things: boy, city, dog, book.
Proper nouns name specific things and start with a capital letter: Ravi, Hyderabad, Monday.
Countable nouns can be counted: one apple, two apples.
Uncountable nouns cannot be counted: water, rice, information, advice.
Collective nouns name a group: team, family, herd, class.
Abstract nouns name ideas or feelings: honesty, love, freedom, happiness.
examples: The dog is barking at the gate. (common noun)
Honesty is the best policy. (abstract noun)
The team won the match. (collective noun)
She drank a glass of water. (uncountable noun)
I have two brothers and one sister. (countable nouns)

commonMistakes.right: I have a lot of furniture.
commonMistakes.why: 'Furniture' is uncountable, so it has no plural form.
'Advice' is uncountable; it cannot take 'an' or a plural 's'.
Proper nouns like 'Delhi' must be capitalized.

quiz.question: Which word is a proper noun?
quiz.explanation: Mumbai is the name of a specific city, so it's a proper noun.

quiz.question: Which is an uncountable noun?
quiz.explanation: Water cannot be counted individually as 'one water, two waters'.

quiz.question: Which is an abstract noun?
quiz.explanation: Honesty is an idea/quality, not a physical object.

quiz.question: The ___ (group of players) celebrated their victory.
quiz.explanation: 'Team' is a collective noun referring to a group.

quiz.sentence: i met my friend in mumbai.
quiz.correction: I met my friend in Mumbai.
quiz.explanation: 'I' and proper nouns like 'Mumbai' must be capitalized.
